title: Our
Story
hero.subtitle: Three decades of devotion to Algerian traditional clothing — worn, cherished, and passed down.

intro.title: Where every piece carries the {{em}}weight of tradition{{/em}}
intro.text: Founded in 1994 by Hadj Mourad Benali, Modeline was born from a passion for Algerian textile heritage — a heritage too beautiful to stay locked in museum glass. It deserved to be worn, celebrated, and handed down through generations. We have spent thirty years bringing that heritage to you.

story.title: A family store, {{em}}rooted in tradition{{/em}}
story.paragraph1: Hadj Mourad discovered his love for traditional Algerian dress in the old medina of Tlemcen. {{strong}}What began as a small boutique in the Casbah of Algiers{{/strong}} has grown into a curated selection from trusted artisan families spanning six wilayas — yet the ethos remains unchanged.
story.paragraph2: We source exclusively from {{strong}}master craftspeople who have inherited their techniques across at least three generations{{/strong}}. Every piece is carefully selected and inspected before reaching you — authenticated, documented, and accompanied by a certificate naming the artisan who made it.
story.paragraph3: This is not fast fashion dressed in traditional motifs. {{strong}}These are living objects{{/strong}}, carrying the specific vocabulary of their region: the braided closures of Tlemcen, the gold-thread arabesques of Constantine, the geometric precision of Ghardaïa. We believe you should know the difference — and so should the people who inherit these pieces from you.

pillars.title: Principles that {{em}}guide every sale{{/em}}
pillars.subtitle: Three commitments that have never wavered since the first day we opened.

items.title: Authentic Provenance
items.desc: Every piece is sourced directly from its regional artisan family. We never use intermediaries, and we never compromise on origin. Each item ships with a signed artisan certificate and a wax-sealed provenance card.

items.title: Fair Trade Economy
items.desc: Artisan families receive no less than 65% of the final sale price — a standard far above the industry norm. We believe the people who carry this knowledge deserve prosperity, not just recognition.

items.title: Living Heritage
items.desc: We invest a portion of every sale into apprenticeship programmes in Tlemcen, Constantine, and Kabylie, ensuring that the techniques behind these garments are passed to the next generation of Algerian hands.

artisans.eyebrow: The Hands Behind What We Sell
artisans.title: Our {{em}}trusted suppliers{{/em}}
artisans.subtitle: The craftspeople whose work fills every corner of our collection.

people.name: Hadj Mourad Benali
people.bio: A fourth-generation connoisseur of traditional Algerian dress who spent years studying the finest burnous traditions of Tlemcen. Hadj Mourad's eye for quality is the foundation of everything Modeline sells.

people.name: Lalla Fatima Chérif
people.bio: Inheritor of the Qasrantina gold-thread tradition, Lalla Fatima has curated Constantine embroidery for the store since 2001. Her bridal chedda ensembles are ordered years in advance.

people.name: Amayas Aït Ouyahia
people.bio: Keeper of traditional Kabyle weaving craft. Amayas supplies the distinctive geometric textiles of Kabylie — their patterns a written language older than the alphabet.

reviews.title: Words from those who {{em}}shop with us{{/em}}
reviews.subtitle: Every review is left by a verified customer. We share them unedited — the praise and the suggestions alike — because trust is only built in the open.
reviews.empty: No reviews yet — be the first to share your experience.
